steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare Kipen's theory with Sarris's. A valid answer must name both theorists' positions and directly contrast them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In repositioning the screenwriter (not the director) as the primary author of a film, schreiber theory presents an alternative to the dominant auteur theory.': Directly contrasts Kipen's schreiber theory (screenwriter as primary author) with Sarris's auteur theory (director as primary author). Both theories are compared. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Film critics Andrew Sarris and David Kipen hold the view that film authorship should be shared among those involved in the filmmaking process.': Misrepresents both theorists — Sarris and Kipen each argue for a single primary author, not shared authorship. That's Kael's position. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In a 1963 rebuttal to Sarris's theory that a director is the primary author of a film, Kael proposed that screenwriters should instead be given sole authorship.': Compares Sarris and Kael, not Sarris and Kipen. Also misrepresents Kael, who argued for shared authorship, not screenwriter sole authorship. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Kipen's theory of film authorship contrasts with that of Kael, who famously argued against Sarris's auteur theory in 1963.': Compares Kipen with Kael, not with Sarris. The required comparison is between Kipen and Sarris. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
